reasoning:
CONFIRMED REAL, with one mechanical correction to the reviewer's trace that does not change the outcome.

Verified in electron/browser/browserGazeController.ts (commit a46113e): (1) The save block (L1310-1322) computes frac purely from wall-clock `now - state.start` with no on-target check â€” the only conditions are !clicked, start>0, targetKey truthy. state.targetKey is never cleared while clickReq is null in the held path (cleared only at gcHide L1066, gcResetDwell L1093, gcBlockDwell L1121, video suppression L1196, onset-cancel L1423 which requires clickReq, and post-click L1561). The commit (L1491) requires clickReq, so with clickReq null the clock overruns dwellMs with no commit and no reset, and `Math.min(0.99, ...)` (L1314) converts arbitrary overrun into a 0.99 save attributed to the stale targetKey. (2) Resume (L1380-1393) reconstructs state.start = now - (0.99*820+280) = now - 1091.8ms against dwellMs=1100 (config L123-125), skipping onset; the commit fires on the second frame after gaze re-lands (~30-45ms at 66Hz), confirmed by step-by-step frame trace (landing frame: reset, targetKey='A', save kept since L1345 only invalidates on a DIFFERENT key; next frame: resume, elapsed 1091.8<1100; following frame: elapsed ~1106.8>1100 â†’ commit). (3) The app contrast is accurate: GazeCursor.tsx saves at the instant the hit-test leaves the target (L826-856) so elapsed is genuinely on-target time, and `currentProgress < 1` (L836) discards overrun instead of clamping â€” the commit message's 'same savedDwellRef semantics' claim is false in both respects.

Correction to the reviewer's mechanics: stabilityHeld's `dist` is NOT frame-to-frame velocity â€” state.x/y are only written in the reset block (L1331-1332), so dist is displacement from the last dwell anchor, and gaze cannot be held with targetKey='A' more than ~240px from the card (180px clickReq unsnap zone, L700-743, + 60px baseStability bubble around the last anchor). The '500ms of dead space' and 'tremor jump needed' details are therefore imprecise. However the corrected trace reaches the same failure: within the 180px locked zone clickReq stays non-null and each displacement break immediately saveâ†’resumes (carrying the clock forward via the new mechanism itself); once gaze crosses 180px from the rect, clickReq goes null, radius reverts to 60px from the last anchor (plantable right at the 180px boundary), and the clock crosses dwellMs off-target with no commit possible â€” the break at the bubble edge saves min(0.99, overrun) for card A. No tremor required; the drift itself breaks at the bubble edge. Example feasible timeline: 700ms on card (frac 0.51 real) + 200ms locked-zone drift (break/resume at 0.756) + ~350ms slow drift through the 60px dead bubble â†’ elapsed 1250ms at break â†’ frac clamped to 0.99 â†’ glance back within 1s TTL â†’ commit ~30ms after gaze lands, faster than human reaction (~200ms), navigating to a video the patient drifted away from.

Pre-change, this clock inflation existed but was harmless on the dominant pattern: any stability break or saccade discarded it (commit requires clickReq). The diff makes the inflated/overrun clock survive a saccade as a near-complete save â€” a new, diff-introduced failure mode.

Would manifest: the trigger pattern (long dwell â†’ slow drift off â†’ glance back within 1s) is the patient's documented dominant behavior per the commit message itself ('60-80% dwell, brief excursion, full restart'), ALS oculomotor drift is slow, and even partial (non-overrun) inflation shortens the glance-back commit well below the intended dwell time. High likelihood in normal YouTube browsing.
suggestedFix: Mirror the app cursor's actual semantics in the injected browser script (electron/browser/browserGazeController.ts): (1) Treat target loss as a break â€” in the held path, when clickReq is null AND the sticky ghost did not fire (i.e., the tracked target can no longer be resolved), immediately perform the save with elapsed snapshotted at that frame, then reset state.start/state.targetKey, exactly like GazeCursor.tsx's !clickable branch (L826-862). This stops the clock at the moment gaze leaves the target's resolvable zone. (2) Discard overrun instead of clamping: replace `const frac = Math.min(0.99, ...)` with a guard `if (frac >= 0.05 && frac < 1)` so an overrun clock is never saved (mirrors GazeCursor's `currentProgress < 1` check at L836). (3) Optionally cap resume so at least one full onset-free warning interval remains before commit, e.g. resume at Math.min(savedProgress, 0.85), guaranteeing >100ms of visible ring before any commit. Add a replay-harness test: dwell 700ms on a card, drift out past the unsnap zone at <150px/s, hold 400ms, saccade back within 1s â€” assert no click commits within 300ms of re-landing.
